purpose: The matured cohort: 18 major schemes launched 2008-2020 whose incentive -> investment -> outcome loop can now be judged, each traced launch-to-outcome INSIDE the PIB register (122,141 releases, 2017-2026) with PRID-cited trajectory waypoints. Extends the ethanol precedent to a full cohort and answers: which instrument designs actually produce trade and forex results in India?
method: Register extended to 2017-2020 by four parallel day-wise backfills (modern portal's boundary is 2017; pre-2017 launches reconstructed from launch dates and baselines stated in later releases -- the ethanol method). Three thematic tracer agents (energy, transport/connectivity, finance/industry) built 3-6-waypoint trajectories per scheme from Parliament-answer and Cabinet releases preferentially; loop verdicts are register-internal. Two further agents classified the 2017-2020 Cabinet registers (61 launches), completing the 2017-2026 launch dataset (154 launches).
headline: Across 18 matured schemes, every CLOSED-SUCCESS loop ran on a demand-side guarantee or procurement design -- administered price + offtake (ethanol), bulk procurement (UJALA's 10x LED price crash), revenue-share auctions (coal), VGF route auctions (UDAN), credit guarantees (MUDRA), capex + tariff protection (M-SIPS/PMP -> mobiles: production 28x, exports 127x, imports 75% of demand -> 0.02%). The two laggards were a voluntary-uptake model (SATAT: 50 of 5,000 plants) and a thrice-redesigned build-out (BharatNet). The 2025-26 instrument wave's return to guaranteed-buyer/guaranteed-terms designs is, in hindsight, a reversion to the proven family.

energy.latest: 20% blending ~5y early; Rs 1.97L cr forex saved; ~316 lakh MT crude substituted (PRID 2283118)
traj: procured 38cr L (ESY14) -> ~140cr L (17-18); admin price since 2014
10% five months early; Rs 41,500cr forex; Rs 40,600cr to farmers
energy.design: administered price + guaranteed OMC offtake + feedstock widening
energy.note: cleanest launch-to-outcome loop in the register

energy.latest: 36.87cr bulbs; Rs 19,153cr annual savings; procurement price Rs ~310 -> Rs 38 (10x crash); 1.03cr streetlights (PRID 2090639)
energy.design: zero-subsidy bulk procurement via EESL crashing prices
energy.link: cut fossil generation; built domestic LED mfg displacing lamp imports
energy.note: direct distribution plateaued vs 77cr ambition, but market transformation exceeded: private sector sold 115+cr LEDs

energy.latest: 50 of 5,000 plants (1%); FY23 offtake 11,227T vs 15 MMT/yr target (<0.1%); pivot to mandatory CBG Blending Obligation from FY26 (PRID 1986252/1979705)
energy.note: largest target-vs-outcome gap in the register; voluntary model failed until mandate

energy.latest: 100.33GW installed Feb-2025 (target was 2022 -- ~3y late); module mfg 2GW (2014) -> 60GW (2024) -> 100GW ALMM-listed (Aug-2025, 21->100 makers) (PRIDs 2100603, 2156173)
energy.design: parks+tenders for capacity; ALMM market-access + PLI for manufacturing
energy.link: explicit import substitution vs Chinese modules; cell/wafer upstream still import-dependent
energy.note: capacity target missed by ~3 years (never stated as a miss in register); manufacturing loop closed emphatically

energy.latest: 15 rolling tranches by Jul-2026; 100+MT captive/commercial output by Nov FY25; Rs 6,656cr/yr state revenue from tranche 1 (PRIDs 2283411, 2072033)
energy.design: revenue-share auctions, no end-use restriction, 100% FDI
energy.note: later tranches thinner bidding but model demonstrably working

energy.latest: 10,203MW of ~34,800MW target (29%) after 7y; Comp-B pumps 9.4L installed carry the gains; Comp-A at 7% of sanctions; 21.77L farmers (PRIDs 2201309, 2244667)
energy.note: pump component works; grid-plant component badly lags

transport_connectivity.latest: 679 routes, 95 aerodromes, 1.68cr passengers, 3.58L flights; national operational airports 74 (2014) -> 165 (Jul-2026); succeeded by Modified UDAN Rs28,840cr + Viksit UDAN launch 04-Jul-2026
transport_connectivity.note: cumulative VGF disbursed never published -- flagged

transport_connectivity.latest: PM E-DRIVE 21.24L EVs sold at 30-12-2025 (e-2W 74% of target; e-3W L5 100%, closed early); extended to 31-Mar-2028
traj: FAME-II close: 13.41L EVs Rs5,790cr; outlay to Rs11,500cr
transport_connectivity.link: crude-import substitution via demand-pull + PLI ecosystem
transport_connectivity.note: succession fully traceable in register incl misuse penalties

transport_connectivity.latest: 2,22,341 GPs undertaken / 2,14,904 service-ready (Dec-2025); Amended BharatNet Rs1,39,579cr in state-led rollout
transport_connectivity.link: services exportability substrate; no forex metric
transport_connectivity.note: three redesigns each reset targets; usage metrics absent

transport_connectivity.latest: 845 projects Rs6.06L cr / 315 complete Rs1.57L cr; port turnaround 96h (2014) -> 49.5h (2025); 915 MT major-port cargo FY26; successor wave = Rs69,725cr shipbuilding package + Sagarmala 2.0 Rs85,482cr
transport_connectivity.link: ports carry 95% of trade volume; turnaround halving cuts export logistics cost
transport_connectivity.note: CEZ pillar quietly faded; execution loop open

transport_connectivity.latest: EDFC complete (Dec-2023); WDFC trucks-on-trains commercial (545 rakes, Rs36.95cr rev FY25 Apr-Dec); final Vaitarana-JNPT leg unevidenced in register
transport_connectivity.link: double-stack to JNPT/Mundra export gateways; transit 30h->12h

transport_connectivity.latest: 1,002 rail cars exported since 2016; 3,800+ bogies, 5,000+ propulsion systems; Marhowra locos to Africa from 2025
transport_connectivity.link: direct forex earner; no consolidated export-value figure in register

latest: 57cr+ loans, Rs 40.07L cr disbursed; women ~60% accounts; 12cr+ new-entrepreneur accounts
finance_industry.note: success on disbursement/inclusion metrics only -- register silent on NPAs

finance_industry.design: DPIIT recognition + FFS Rs10,000cr via AIFs + seed fund + guarantees
trajectory: Rs7,980cr to 99 AIFs; Rs14,077cr into 791 startups
latest: FFS 1.0 fully committed (145 AIFs, Rs25,500cr+ into 1,370+ startups) -> FoF 2.0 Rs10,000cr notified; record 55,200 recognitions FY26

finance_industry.design: 20-25% capex subsidy era -> production-linked era -> brand era
trajectory: Rs18,000cr -> Rs5.45L cr (28x); exports Rs1,500cr -> Rs2L cr (127x); imports 75% of demand -> 0.02%; LSEM cumulative prod Rs8.45L cr, exports Rs4.66L cr, 1.30L jobs
latest: LSEM tenure ended; MPMS Rs62,500cr successor; 99.2% of phones used in India made domestically; smartphones #1 export category 2025
finance_industry.link: largest single trade-balance swing in the register (net importer -> net exporter)
finance_industry.note: CORRECTION: '121% of export target' not found in register -- use register-native figures

finance_industry.link: agri-export infra; export throughput never quantified
finance_industry.note: ~58% operational after 15 years; quiet discontinuation after third-party evaluation

finance_industry.design: assured-return gas-linked pricing for new/revived capacity
trajectory: 6 new units of 12.7 LMT each (4 PSU JV + 2 private incl Matix)
latest: NIPU-2026 as explicit extension (RoE 12-16%); 33 units/269.42 LMT capacity but gap still import-filled
finance_industry.link: direct import substitution; self-sufficiency missed -> NIPU

finance_industry.design: umbrella facilitation, no own fiscal instrument
latest: 11 years; FY25 FDI $81.04bn -- plateau since FY21; EoDB 142->63
finance_industry.link: FDI is the register's hardest forex-side outcome

cross_cutting_findings: Design beats sector: successes and failures appear in the same sectors (solar capacity PARTIAL but solar manufacturing loop closed; FAME demand-pull succeeded while ACC production-payout stalled) -- the instrument, not the industry, predicts closure.

Voluntary-uptake models fail quietly: SATAT's 3,694 LOIs -> ~50 plants and Mega Food Parks' 24/41 operational both ended in course-corrections (mandatory blending obligation; discontinuation) that the launch releases never anticipated.

Misses are never announced: solar's 2022 target passed without a miss statement (achieved 2025); Food Parks were discontinued in a line of a Parliament answer; SATAT's pivot to mandates is dated but unheralded. The register records outcomes; it does not confess.

The employment-linked design has deep roots: PMRPY (2018, full EPF subsidy) and ABRY (2020, Rs 22,810cr) prefigure ELI 2025 -- the payout base's migration to jobs is a decade-old thread, not an invention.

Trade-balance swings concentrate: mobiles (net importer -> #1 export) and ethanol (Rs 1.97L cr forex saved) dwarf every other traced outcome -- both took ~8-10 years from instrument to closed loop, the honest timescale for the 2026 wave's bets (gasification, NIPU, critical minerals).

CAVEATS: loop verdicts are register-internal (PIB's own figures; MUDRA judged on disbursement not credit quality); pre-2017 launches traced through later releases; celebratory releases used only for milestone counts, Parliament answers preferred.
see_also: scratchpad classifications 2017-18 (28 launches) and 2019-20 (33 launches) folded into the quarterly series
